Browse Topics Search

Making it work

Penny Loeb U.S. News & World Report September 1996
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsJournalism & Newsroom

A U.S. News editor reflects on the publication's computer-assisted reporting efforts, arguing that CAR should be driven by beat reporters rather than database specialists to be most effective.

In the three-and-a-half years I've been at U.S. News, we've done about 70 computer-assisted stories... But ultimately, it's not the responsibility of the database editor to make good CAR stories happen. If we're going to do this right, CAR has to come organically out of the work of the beat reporter.
